Title: France celebrates Bastille Day, Macron's last as president, with parade showcasing unity with allies and Ukraine
Description: The final July 14 parade presided over by President Emmanuel Macron, the event brought together thousands of French troops but also two dozen foreign heads of state and government, including Ukrainian President Volodymyr Zelensky.

Title: 2026 World Cup: Ahead of semifinal against Spain, France's unity is its superpower

How the 2016 Nice terrorist attack unfolded, minute-by-minute
Description: On July 14, 2016, a terrorist drove a truck into crowds gathered on the famed Promenade des Anglais in the southern French city, killing 86 people. Ten years later, Le Monde looks back at how the tragedy unfolded.

Title: Hungarian parliament votes to remove president in controversial constitutional reform
Description: Initiated by the party of Prime Minister Péter Magyar, the reform, which includes the  immediate termination of President Tamas Sulyok's term, also bars two-thirds of Fidesz MPs from running for Parliament in the next elections, set for 2030.

Title: 'Immigration is beneficial for the economy as a whole. Why is it so unpopular?'
Description: A new study highlights the economic benefits of immigration in OECD countries. These findings, which have been repeated for decades, unfortunately do not shift the debate, says Eric Albert in his column.

Title: Macron and Zelensky, a special relationship marked by misunderstandings
Description: Ukrainian soldiers will join France's annual Bastille Day military parade in Paris on Tuesday, July 14. French President Emmanuel Macron and his Ukrainian counterpart Volodymyr Zelensky will stand together in a sign of Paris's support for Kyiv, but their relationship has not always been so straightforward.

Title: 'And I thought coke was for stressed-out executives!': Cocaine's ravages among manual workers
Description: 'Cocaine at work' (3/3). Long associated with elite, urban circles, cocaine has now made its way deep into rural France and its mid-sized towns. On construction sites, farms and in warehouses, workers, some barely out of their teens, are turning to the drug to get through the physical toll of their jobs.

Title: Martine Barrat, a photographer without fear or limits
Description: At 93, the artist has lost none of her vitality or curiosity. She lives at the legendary Hotel Chelsea in New York, where she remains one of the last year-round artist residents. Today, her work is the subject of a retrospective at the Rencontres d'Arles photography festival.
